metadata.title: Base QA test review rubric
metadata.description: Default severity rules for /gaia-qa-tests covering assertion quality, test isolation, coverage, flakiness, and naming. Layer 1 of the four-layer rubric composition.

severity_rules.pattern: Test ends with a side-effect call but no assertion — exercises the code path without verifying the outcome.
severity_rules.description: Tests without assertions only catch crashes; behavioural regressions slip through silently.
severity_rules.remediation: Add explicit assertions on observable outcomes (return value, side-effect on a collaborator, persisted state).

severity_rules.pattern: Test asserts only on truthiness (`expect(result).toBeTruthy()`) or non-nullness without verifying the actual expected value.
severity_rules.description: Truthiness-only assertions accept many incorrect outputs and erode the test's signal.
severity_rules.remediation: Assert on the specific expected value, type, or shape — never just truthiness as the sole assertion.

severity_rules.pattern: Single test contains more than one logically-distinct behavioural assertion (multiple act-and-assert cycles in one case).
severity_rules.description: Multi-assertion tests fail at the first failure and hide the second behaviour, slowing diagnosis.
severity_rules.remediation: Split each behavioural assertion into its own test case with descriptive naming.

severity_rules.pattern: Test depends on order — passes only if a prior test ran first, or mutates a shared singleton without resetting it.
severity_rules.description: Order-dependent tests are flaky under parallel execution and obscure root cause when one fails in isolation.
severity_rules.remediation: Move shared setup into `beforeEach` / `setUp` and reset all mutable state between cases.

severity_rules.pattern: Test reads from or writes to a shared external resource (real database, real network, real filesystem outside the per-test temp dir) without isolation.
severity_rules.description: Real-resource tests are non-hermetic — they fail in CI for environment reasons and pollute state across runs.
severity_rules.remediation: Stub the external resource, route through a per-test container, or use a temp dir scoped to the test case.

severity_rules.pattern: Acceptance criterion or new public function has no corresponding test — coverage of the changed surface is incomplete.
severity_rules.description: Untested ACs and entry points let regressions reach production without warning.
severity_rules.remediation: Add at least one happy-path and one failure-path test for every AC and every new public entry point.

severity_rules.pattern: Critical branch or error path is untested — exception handlers, retry loops, and authorisation denials run only in production.
severity_rules.description: Untested error paths reverse the protective intent of the handler — bugs in the recovery code are masked until the failure mode hits production.
severity_rules.remediation: Add a test that triggers the failure mode and asserts on the handler's observable outcome (rethrow, fallback, structured error).

severity_rules.pattern: Test relies on a real `setTimeout` / `sleep` for synchronisation instead of awaiting a deterministic event or fake-clock advance.
severity_rules.description: Sleep-based synchronisation creates wall-clock races; the test passes locally and flakes in CI under load.
severity_rules.remediation: Wait on the actual event (promise resolution, observable emission, callback) or advance a fake clock.

severity_rules.pattern: Test uses non-deterministic input (`Date.now()`, `Math.random()`, `os.urandom`) without seeding or freezing.
severity_rules.description: Non-determinism in test inputs surfaces as intermittent failures and complicates root-cause analysis.
severity_rules.remediation: Inject a seeded RNG or a frozen clock; assert against the seeded output.

severity_rules.pattern: Test asserts on collection ordering when the producer makes no ordering guarantee (Set iteration, hashmap traversal, async result fan-in).
severity_rules.description: Order-dependent assertions over unordered producers are flaky — the assertion passes by coincidence on some runs.
severity_rules.remediation: Sort the actual and expected collections before comparison, or assert on set membership.

severity_rules.pattern: Test name describes mechanism (`it('test 1', ...)`, `test_function_a`) rather than the behaviour under verification.
severity_rules.description: Mechanism-named tests force a reader to open the body to learn intent; behaviour-first names self-document.
severity_rules.remediation: Rename to a sentence describing the behaviour (e.g., `returns 401 when the token is expired`).

severity_rules.pattern: Test name omits the precondition, action, or expected outcome — fails the Given-When-Then sniff test.
severity_rules.description: Names that omit a Given-When-Then triplet are harder to scan in a test report.
severity_rules.remediation: Adopt a `<scenario>__<action>__<outcome>` or `should ... when ...` convention across the suite.
